title: align first-task proof assets with meaningful backlog-task positioning
highest_leverage_reason: Adoption Funnel says the strongest next asset is a Start Here / first-task guide because the current bottleneck is conversion from interest to free use.
The existing example proof assets contradicted that positioning by using a tiny validation task as the flagship first example.
Fixing that contradiction improves repo conversion surfaces immediately without adding more same-family outreach or directory overlap during active measurement windows.
change_summary: Replaced the tiny CLI validation example with a meaningful billing-history product slice that better matches Ralph Workflow's 'too big to babysit, still reviewable' positioning.
Aligned the review-bundle example to the same substantial task so evaluators see a coherent morning-after proof story instead of a toy change.
Kept Codeberg-first calls to action intact in both assets.
expected_outcome: Higher-quality repo conversion by making the first proof assets support the product's real use case instead of underselling it with a trivial demo.

measurement_window.success_metric: Next adoption/content audit no longer flags trivial-first-task proof drift and Codeberg conversion surfaces remain aligned around meaningful first runs.
measurement_window.secondary_metric: Any new distribution or site CTA can point to a stronger first-task example without contradicting README/START_HERE framing.

replacement_condition: If Codeberg adoption and evaluator movement remain flat after aligned proof assets and active distribution windows age in, stop treating first-task proof alignment as enough and switch the next run to a distinct external demand-capture lane with live placement.
